lastUpdateSummary.headline: Another 2 million barrels cleared Iranian export channels while visible Hormuz traffic stayed subdued.
lastUpdateSummary.body: A TankerTrackers post on 15 March says another 2 million barrels of Iranian crude departed even as the strait remained very quiet. For the dashboard, that strengthens the emerging read from the prior Windward and Maritime Executive coverage: low visible crossings are not the same as zero effective flow, because Iranian-linked liftings can still clear cargo under selective or lower-visibility conditions while mainstream Gulf tanker traffic remains heavily suppressed.

last24hSummary.headline: The last 24 hours still point to selective, politically filtered flow rather than a broad reopening.
last24hSummary.body: Across the sources reviewed, the pattern remains consistent: zero or near-zero visible crossings can coexist with continued Kharg liftings, selective approvals for favoured operators, dark or partially visible movement, and a large waiting fleet outside the strait. The most useful fresh confirmation this round is that Iranian-linked crude is still clearing in cargo-sized increments even while compliant commercial traffic remains exceptionally thin.
